Strategy Name: Federal Open Market Committee Meeting Effect in Stocks
Strategy Description: A market timing strategy that goes long on stocks only during Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meeting days.
Core Logic: FOMC meetings are generally positive for the market as the Fed aims to maintain stability and growth, leading to a positive drift in stock prices.
Entry Conditions: Go long (e.g., S&P 500 ETF) at the close one day before the FOMC meeting.
Exit Conditions: Close the position at the end of the meeting day.
Risk Management Rules: Invested in cash during all non-meeting days; low market exposure (approx. 8 days per year).
Market Regime Suitability: Not a hedge for bear markets; exploits specific event-driven optimism.
Volatility Considerations: Low overall portfolio volatility due to high cash allocation.
Full Notes: More than 16% of average yearly returns for equity indices are realized during these few days. The strategy can be easily leveraged.
Mathematical/Statistical Concepts: Calendar Effect, Drift Analysis, Event Study.
